2606.11482.title: Building Social World Models with Large Language Models
2606.11482.paper_summary: Understanding and predicting how social beliefs evolve in response to events -- from policy changes to scientific breakthroughs -- remains a fundamental challenge in social science. Given LLMs' commonsense knowledge and social intelligence, we ask: Can LLMs model the dynamics of social beliefs following social events? In this work, we introduce the concept of the Social World Model (SWM), a general framework designed to capture how social beliefs evolve in response to major events. SWM learns state-transition functions for social beliefs by mining temporal patterns in social data and optimizing the evidence lower bound, without the need for explicit human annotations linking events to belief shifts, or for expensive census data. To evaluate SWM, we introduce a benchmark, SWM-bench, derived from real-world prediction markets, specifically Kalshi and Polymarket. SWM-bench includes over 12k data points for social belief prediction tasks spanning diverse domains such as politics, finance, and cryptocurrency. Our experimental results show that SWM significantly outperforms time-series foundation models, achieving state-of-the-art results on Kalshi data and demonstrating competitive performance on Polymarket data, while offering interpretable insights into the underlying mechanisms of social belief dynamics.
2606.11482.paper_summary_zh: 理解和預測社會信念如何隨著事件（從政策變更到科學突破）而演變，仍然是社會科學中的一個基本挑戰。考慮到LLMs的常識知識和社會智慧，我們提出：LLMs能否模擬社會事件後社會信念的動態？在這項工作中，我們引入了社會世界模型（SWM）的概念，這是一個旨在捕捉社會信念如何隨重大事件而演變的通用框架。SWM通過挖掘社會數據中的時間模式並優化證據下界，學習社會信念的狀態轉換函數，而無需明確的人類註釋將事件與信念變化聯繫起來，也無需昂貴的普查數據。為了評估SWM，我們引入了一個基準，SWM-bench，該基準源自現實世界的預測市場，特別是Kalshi和Polymarket。SWM-bench包含超過12,000個數據點，用於涵蓋政治、金融和加密貨幣等多個領域的社會信念預測任務。我們的實驗結果顯示，SWM的表現顯著優於時間序列基礎模型，在Kalshi數據上達到最先進的結果，並在Polymarket數據上展現出競爭力的表現，同時提供了對社會信念動態基本機制的可解釋見解。
2606.11482.author: Haofei Yu et.al.
2606.11482.authors: Haofei Yu, Yining Zhao, Guanyu Lin, Jiaxuan You
